name: Fluid Sim 3D
description:
Volumetric 3D particle fluid simulation, fully atom-decomposed and organized into named node groups for legibility. The groups are a presentation layer that flattens away byte-identical at load; card bindings address inner nodes by stable nodeId, so grouping does not touch the performance surface or behavior.

The top level shows the feedback loop and primary data flow: Inputs deliver canvas dimensions and time; Camera Control orients the view; Particle State (array_feedback) holds last frame's simulation state. Spawn Particles and Seed Pattern allocate the buffer and generate patterns on reset. Move Particles (the per-particle integrator chain) cycles last frame's particles through force sampling, turbulence, diffusion, container repulsion, Euler integration, boundary containment, camera-plane flattening, and injection. Render Volume splats particles into a 3D density texture. Force Field converts density to force via central-difference gradient and curl/slope operations, then blurs the field. Display renders particles to screen via camera projection, tone-maps, and scales to output.

Clip Triggers is the performance engine: a clip trigger fires a decay envelope; the Trigger Mode dial routes that envelope to exactly one of five nested behaviors — Turbulence Burst (amplify noise), Curl Rotation (rotate force field), Flow Inversion (flip gradient), Pattern Reset (reseed buffer), Inject Burst (add particles at zone) — which is why those five live as sub-groups sharing one envelope and mode selector.

Resolution Scaling derives canvas-dependent numbers: particle count, density scatter energy, blur radii, display intensity and zoom.

Container Control manages the three shape constraints (Cube, Sphere, Torus) that bound particles during seeding, force repulsion, and hard boundary enforcement.

Known deltas vs. the legacy fused build are documented in the preset description above the graph. The decomposition unlocks per-stage reuse: every integrator atom (sample, noise, diffuse, repel, euler, bounds, flatten, inject) is available for any 3D particle sim; seed patterns are JSON-editable WGSL; the force-field assembly is reusable for any volumetric flow. The groups flatten to byte-identical behavior.

nodes.title: Seed Pattern 3D
